metadata.name: Information Delivery Process
metadata.description: Delivery-phase information management workflow defined in ISO 19650-2:2018.
metadata.author: ISO19650 Knowledge Maps

nodes.label: Assessment of Need
nodes.description: Initial stage where the appointing party assesses project or asset needs and defines information requirements.

nodes.label: Invitation to Tender
nodes.description: Stage where information requirements are issued to potential suppliers.

nodes.label: Tender Response
nodes.description: Suppliers respond with proposed information delivery capabilities and plans.

nodes.label: Appointment
nodes.description: The appointing party appoints the delivery team.

nodes.label: Mobilization
nodes.description: Teams prepare systems, resources, and the common data environment.

nodes.label: Collaborative Production of Information
nodes.description: Delivery teams produce and coordinate information during the project.

nodes.label: Information Model Delivery
nodes.description: Delivery of the project information model at defined exchange milestones.

nodes.label: Project Closeout
nodes.description: Completion of delivery and transition of information to asset management.

nodes.label: Information Model
nodes.description: Structured collection of information containers used to support decisions.

nodes.label: Common Data Environment
nodes.description: Shared information environment used to manage and exchange information.

evidence.quote: The appointing party assesses the need and defines information requirements prior to inviting tenders.
Potential suppliers respond to the invitation to tender with proposals for information delivery.
Following evaluation of tender responses, the appointing party appoints the selected suppliers.
Following appointment, teams mobilize to prepare for information production.
Mobilization establishes the systems and resources required for collaborative production of information.
Information models are progressively developed and delivered at defined information exchange milestones.
Information is delivered at project closeout to support asset management.
Information containers collectively form the project information model.
The common data environment is the agreed source of information for the project.
